ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class (FOODON_03301339); no specific peach-flavoured black tea entry exists. Closest parent match used; peach flavouring and no-topping variant documented here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers black tea beverage generically. No specific peach-flavoured or topping-free variant code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for peach-flavoured black tea beverage without topping. Null used to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: flavoured with peach syrup or peach flavouring

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of peach black tea without topping, approximately 350 ml (350 g), as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea and specialty beverage outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Peach Black Tea (No Topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Peach Black Tea (No Topping), CR ID D08010086. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Fields not reported by HPB (monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) are set to -1 per Veritas numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for sugar-sweetened tea beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peach black tea (no topping) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the analogous food category of sugar-sweetened tea beverages, consistent with sucrose-dominant beverages reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Carbohydrate per serving (350g × 7.49g/100g = 26.2g carbohydrate). GL = (60 × 26.2) / 100 = 15.7. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for precision.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49g sugar per 100g; sodium is negligible at 1mg/100g but added sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as regular consumption of added sugar at this concentration (7.49g/100g) is independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines; request an unsweetened or zero-sugar version instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49g sugar per 100g, zero dietary fibre, and medium GI (~60); liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, producing acute postprandial glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes because it is a sugar-sweetened drink with 7.49g sugar per 100g and zero fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; individuals should choose genuinely unsweetened black tea or request a zero-sugar formulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.49g per 100g contributes to excess caloric intake and triglyceride elevation; fat and saturated fat are zero, which is favourable, but the SSB classification remains a cardiovascular risk factor
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as habitual SSB consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk per the American Heart Association; an unsweetened black tea variant would be a clinically preferable alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.49g per 100g with zero dietary fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as liquid sugar at this concentration promotes inflammatory mediators without any offsetting anti-inflammatory benefit; switching to unsweetened peach black tea would allow access to black tea theaflavin polyphenols without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Black Tea (No Topping) is a commercially prepared sugar-sweetened specialty tea beverage widely available at Singaporean bubble tea and specialty drink outlets. It is made from freshly brewed black tea combined with peach flavouring or peach syrup, served without any customisable toppings such as pearls, jelly, or pudding. The beverage has a light amber colour, a mild astringent black tea base, and a sweet, fruity peach aroma and flavour. Per 100g, it provides 29 kcal, 7.49g carbohydrate (entirely as sugar), 0.01g protein, 0g fat, 0g dietary fibre, and 1mg sodium. The absence of toppings reduces total caloric load compared to topped variants but does not alter the sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) classification. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to rapid postprandial glucose absorption from liquid sugar with zero fibre buffering. It is also a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management because habitual SSB consumption independently raises blood pressure and triglyceride levels. The beverage is low in sodium, fat-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal-suitable. Black tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins) are present in the brewed base but are insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory and glycaemic impact of the added sugar at this concentration. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010086).

Is peach black tea suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010086) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from an approved peer-reviewed source, clearly documented.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by HPB source data for this beverage formulation; this is a sugar-water-tea base with no fat-containing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same rationale. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as brewed tea beverages contain no intact dietary fibre. protein_g is 0.01g (trace), not zero. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because these fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item and no supplementary approved source was available to fill them. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on sucrose-dominant sugar-sweetened tea beverages per Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care; direct GI measurement for this specific product is recommended. The 'no topping' variant removes pearls, jelly, and pudding but does not alter the beverage base composition or SSB classification.
